NKJV.1: Moreover, brethren, we make known to you the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia:
NKJV.2: that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded in the riches of their liberality.
NKJV.3: For I bear witness that according to their ability, yes, and beyond their ability, they were freely willing,
NKJV.4: imploring us with much urgency that we would receive the gift and the fellowship of the ministering to the saints.
NKJV.5: And not only as we had hoped, but they first gave themselves to the Lord, and then to us by the will of God.
NKJV.6: So we urged Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also complete this grace in you as well.
NKJV.7: But as you abound in everything—in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in all diligence, and in your love for us—see that you abound in this grace also.
NKJV.8: I speak not by commandment, but I am testing the sincerity of your love by the diligence of others.
NKJV.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though He was rich, yet for your sakes He became poor, that you through His poverty might become rich.
NKJV.10: And in this I give advice: It is to your advantage not only to be doing what you began and were desiring to do a year ago;
NKJV.11: but now you also must complete the doing of it; that as there was a readiness to desire it, so there also may be a completion out of what you have.
NKJV.12: For if there is first a willing mind, it is accepted according to what one has, and not according to what he does not have.
NKJV.13: For I do not mean that others should be eased and you burdened;
NKJV.14: but by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may supply their lack, that their abundance also may supply your lack—that there may be equality.
NKJV.15: As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.”
NKJV.16: But thanks be to God who puts the same earnest care for you into the heart of Titus.
NKJV.17: For he not only accepted the exhortation, but being more diligent, he went to you of his own accord.
NKJV.18: And we have sent with him the brother whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches,
NKJV.19: and not only that, but who was also chosen by the churches to travel with us with this gift, which is administered by us to the glory of the Lord Himself and to show your ready mind,
NKJV.20: avoiding this: that anyone should blame us in this lavish gift which is administered by us—
NKJV.21: providing honorable things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
NKJV.22: And we have sent with them our brother whom we have often proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, because of the great confidence which we have in you.
NKJV.23: If anyone inquires about Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker concerning you. Or if our brethren are inquired about, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.
NKJV.24: Therefore show to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love and of our boasting on your behalf.

ESV.1: We want you to know, brothers,  about the grace of God that has been given among the churches of Macedonia,
ESV.2: for in a severe test of affliction, their abundance of joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
ESV.3: For they gave according to their means, as I can testify, and beyond their means, of their own accord,
ESV.4: begging us earnestly for the favor  of taking part in the relief of the saints —
ESV.5: and this, not as we expected, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and then by the will of God to us.
ESV.6: Accordingly, we urged Titus that as he had started, so he should complete among you this act of grace.
ESV.7: But as you excel in everything — in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in all earnestness, and in our love for you  — see that you excel in this act of grace also.
ESV.8: I say this not as a command, but to prove by the earnestness of others that your love also is genuine.
ESV.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sake he became poor, so that you by his poverty might become rich.
ESV.10: And in this matter I give my judgment: this benefits you, who a year ago started not only to do this work but also to desire to do it.
ESV.11: So now finish doing it as well, so that your readiness in desiring it may be matched by your completing it out of what you have.
ESV.12: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what a person has, not according to what he does not have.
ESV.13: For I do not mean that others should be eased and you burdened, but that as a matter of fairness
ESV.14: your abundance at the present time should supply their need, so that their abundance may supply your need, that there may be fairness.
ESV.15: As it is written, “Whoever gathered much had nothing left over, and whoever gathered little had no lack.”
ESV.16: But thanks be to God, who put into the heart of Titus the same earnest care I have for you.
ESV.17: For he not only accepted our appeal, but being himself very earnest he is going  to you of his own accord.
ESV.18: With him we are sending  the brother who is famous among all the churches for his preaching of the gospel.
ESV.19: And not only that, but he has been appointed by the churches to travel with us as we carry out this act of grace that is being ministered by us, for the glory of the Lord himself and to show our good will.
ESV.20: We take this course so that no one should blame us about this generous gift that is being administered by us,
ESV.21: for we aim at what is honorable not only in the Lord's sight but also in the sight of man.
ESV.22: And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found earnest in many matters, but who is now more earnest than ever because of his great confidence in you.
ESV.23: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for your benefit. And as for our brothers, they are messengers  of the churches, the glory of Christ.
ESV.24: So give proof before the churches of your love and of our boasting about you to these men.

NASB.1: Now, brethren, we [wish to] make known to you the grace of God which has been given in the churches of Macedonia,
NASB.2: that in a great ordeal of affliction their abundance of joy and their deep poverty overflowed in the wealth of their liberality.
NASB.3: For I testify that according to their ability, and beyond their ability, [they gave] of their own accord,
NASB.4: begging us with much urging for the favor of participation in the support of the saints,
NASB.5: and [this], not as we had expected, but they first gave themselves to the Lord and to us by the will of God.
NASB.6: So we urged Titus that as he had previously made a beginning, so he would also complete in you this gracious work as well.
NASB.7: But just as you abound in everything, in faith and utterance and knowledge and in all earnestness and in the love we inspired in you, [see] that you abound in this gracious work also.
NASB.8: I am not speaking [this] as a command, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity of your love also.
NASB.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though He was rich, yet for your sake He became poor, so that you through His poverty might become rich.
NASB.10: I give [my] opinion in this matter, for this is to your advantage, who were the first to begin a year ago not only to do [this], but also to desire [to do it].
NASB.11: But now finish doing it also, so that just as [there was] the readiness to desire it, so [there] [may be] also the completion of it by your ability.
NASB.12: For if the readiness is present, it is acceptable according to what [a person] has, not according to what he does not have.
NASB.13: For [this] is not for the ease of others [and] for your affliction, but by way of equality--
NASB.14: at this present time your abundance [being a] [supply] for their need, so that their abundance also may become [a supply] for your need, that there may be equality;
NASB.15: as it is written, 'HE WHO [gathered] MUCH DID NOT HAVE TOO MUCH, AND HE WHO [gathered] LITTLE HAD NO LACK.'
NASB.16: But thanks be to God who puts the same earnestness on your behalf in the heart of Titus.
NASB.17: For he not only accepted our appeal, but being himself very earnest, he has gone to you of his own accord.
NASB.18: We have sent along with him the brother whose fame in [the things of] the gospel [has spread] through all the churches;
NASB.19: and not only [this], but he has also been appointed by the churches to travel with us in this gracious work, which is being administered by us for the glory of the Lord Himself, and [to show] our readiness,
NASB.20: taking precaution so that no one will discredit us in our administration of this generous gift;
NASB.21: for we have regard for what is honorable, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
NASB.22: We have sent with them our brother, whom we have often tested and found diligent in many things, but now even more diligent because of [his] great confidence in you.
NASB.23: As for Titus, [he is] my partner and fellow worker among you; as for our brethren, [they are] messengers of the churches, a glory to Christ.
NASB.24: Therefore openly before the churches, show them the proof of your love and of our reason for boasting about you.

NLT.1: Now I want you to know, dear brothers and sisters, what God in his kindness has done through the churches in Macedonia.
NLT.2: They are being tested by many troubles, and they are very poor. But they are also filled with abundant joy, which has overflowed in rich generosity.
NLT.3: For I can testify that they gave not only what they could afford, but far more. And they did it of their own free will.
NLT.4: They begged us again and again for the privilege of sharing in the gift for the believers in Jerusalem.
NLT.5: They even did more than we had hoped, for their first action was to give themselves to the Lord and to us, just as God wanted them to do.
NLT.6: So we have urged Titus, who encouraged your giving in the first place, to return to you and encourage you to finish this ministry of giving.
NLT.7: Since you excel in so many ways — in your faith, your gifted speakers, your knowledge, your enthusiasm, and your love from us — I want you to excel also in this gracious act of giving.
NLT.8: I am not commanding you to do this. But I am testing how genuine your love is by comparing it with the eagerness of the other churches.
NLT.9: You know the generous grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, so that by his poverty he could make you rich.
NLT.10: Here is my advice: It would be good for you to finish what you started a year ago. Last year you were the first who wanted to give, and you were the first to begin doing it.
NLT.11: Now you should finish what you started. Let the eagerness you showed in the beginning be matched now by your giving. Give in proportion to what you have.
NLT.12: Whatever you give is acceptable if you give it eagerly. And give according to what you have, not what you don’t have.
NLT.13: Of course, I don’t mean your giving should make life easy for others and hard for yourselves. I only mean that there should be some equality.
NLT.14: Right now you have plenty and can help those who are in need. Later, they will have plenty and can share with you when you need it. In this way, things will be equal.
NLT.15:
As the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered a lot had nothing left over,
and those who gathered only a little had enough.”
NLT.16: But thank God! He has given Titus the same enthusiasm for you that I have.
NLT.17: Titus welcomed our request that he visit you again. In fact, he himself was very eager to go and see you.
NLT.18: We are also sending another brother with Titus. All the churches praise him as a preacher of the Good News.
NLT.19: He was appointed by the churches to accompany us as we take the offering to Jerusalem — a service that glorifies the Lord and shows our eagerness to help.
NLT.20: We are traveling together to guard against any criticism for the way we are handling this generous gift.
NLT.21: We are careful to be honorable before the Lord, but we also want everyone else to see that we are honorable.
NLT.22: We are also sending with them another of our brothers who has proven himself many times and has shown on many occasions how eager he is. He is now even more enthusiastic because of his great confidence in you.
NLT.23: If anyone asks about Titus, say that he is my partner who works with me to help you. And the brothers with him have been sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
NLT.24: So show them your love, and prove to all the churches that our boasting about you is justified.

WEB.1: Moreover, brothers, we make known to you the grace of God which has been given in the assemblies of Macedonia;
WEB.2: how that in much proof of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded to the riches of their liberality.
WEB.3: For according to their power, I testify, yes and beyond their power, they gave of their own accord,
WEB.4: begging us with much entreaty to receive this grace and the fellowship in the service to the saints.
WEB.5: This was not as we had hoped, but first they gave their own selves to the Lord, and to us through the will of God.
WEB.6: So we urged Titus, that as he had made a beginning before, so he would also complete in you this grace.
WEB.7: But as you abound in everything, in faith, utterance, knowledge, all earnestness, and in your love to us, see that you also abound in this grace.
WEB.8: I speak not by way of commandment, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity also of your love.
WEB.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich.
WEB.10: I give a judgment in this: for this is expedient for you, who were the first to start a year ago, not only to do, but also to be willing.
WEB.11: But now complete the doing also, that as there was the readiness to be willing, so there may be the completion also out of your ability.
WEB.12: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what you have, not according to what you don’t have.
WEB.13: For this is not that others may be eased and you distressed,
WEB.14: but for equality. Your abundance at this present time supplies their lack, that their abundance also may become a supply for your lack; that there may be equality.
WEB.15: As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.”
WEB.16: But thanks be to God, who puts the same earnest care for you into the heart of Titus.
WEB.17: For he indeed accepted our exhortation, but being himself very earnest, he went out to you of his own accord.
WEB.18: We have sent together with him the brother whose praise in the Good News is known through all the assemblies.
WEB.19: Not only so, but who was also appointed by the assemblies to travel with us in this grace, which is served by us to the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our readiness.
WEB.20: We are avoiding this, that any man should blame us concerning this abundance which is administered by us.
WEB.21: Having regard for honorable things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
WEB.22: We have sent with them our brother, whom we have many times proved earnest in many things, but now much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence which he has in you.
WEB.23: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for you. As for our brothers, they are the apostles of the assemblies, the glory of Christ.
WEB.24: Therefore show the proof of your love to them in front of the assemblies, and of our boasting on your behalf.

CSB17.1: We want you to know, brothers and sisters, about the grace of God that was given to the churches <sup>ⓝ</sup> of Macedonia: <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.2: During a severe trial brought about by affliction, their abundant joy and their extreme poverty overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.3: I can testify that, according to their ability and even beyond their ability, of their own accord,
CSB17.4: they begged us earnestly for the privilege of sharing <sup>ⓒ</sup> in the ministry to the saints,
CSB17.5: and not just as we had hoped. Instead, they gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us by God’s will. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.6: So we urged Titus that just as he had begun, so he should also complete among you this act of grace.
CSB17.7: Now as you excel in everything — in faith, speech, knowledge, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and in all diligence, and in your love for us <sup>[1]</sup>  — excel also in this act of grace.
CSB17.8: I am not saying this as a command. Rather, by means of the diligence of others, I am testing the genuineness of your love.
CSB17.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ: Though he was rich, <sup>ⓕ</sup> for your sake he became poor, <sup>ⓖ</sup> so that by his poverty you might become rich.
CSB17.10: And in this matter I am giving advice because it is profitable for you, who began last year not only to do something but also to want to do it. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.11: Now also finish the task, so that just as there was an eager desire, there may also be a completion, according to what you have.
CSB17.12: For if the eagerness is there, the gift is acceptable according to what a person has, not according to what he does not have. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.13: It is not that there should be relief for others and hardship for you, but it is a question of equality. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.14: At the present time your surplus is available for their need, so that their abundance may in turn meet your need, in order that there may be equality.
CSB17.15: As it is written: The person who had much did not have too much, and the person who had little did not have too little. [3] ⓙ
CSB17.16: Thanks be to God, who put the same concern for you into the heart of Titus.
CSB17.17: For he welcomed our appeal and, being very diligent, went out to you by his own choice.
CSB17.18: We have sent with him the brother <sup>ⓚ</sup> who is praised among all the churches for his gospel ministry. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.19: And not only that, but he was also appointed by the churches to accompany us with this gracious gift that we are administering for the glory of the Lord himself and to show our eagerness to help.
CSB17.20: We are taking this precaution so that no one will criticize us about this large sum that we are administering.
CSB17.21: Indeed, we are giving careful thought <sup>ⓛ</sup> to do what is right, <sup>ⓜ</sup> not only before the Lord but also before people.
CSB17.22: We have also sent with them our brother. We have often tested him in many circumstances and found him to be diligent — and now even more diligent because of his great confidence in you.
CSB17.23: As for Titus, he is my partner <sup>ⓝ</sup> and coworker <sup>ⓐ</sup> for you; as for our brothers, they are the messengers <sup>ⓑ</sup> of the churches, the glory of Christ. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.24: Therefore, show them proof before the churches of your love and of our boasting <sup>ⓓ</sup> about you.

AMP.1: Now, brothers and sisters, we want to tell you about the grace of God which has been evident in the churches of Macedonia [awakening in them a longing to contribute];
AMP.2: for during an ordeal of severe distress, their abundant joy and their deep poverty [together] overflowed in the wealth of their lavish generosity.
AMP.3: For I testify that according to their ability, and beyond their ability, they gave voluntarily,
AMP.4: begging us insistently for the privilege of participating in the service for [the support of] the saints [in Jerusalem].
AMP.5: Not only [did they give materially] as we had hoped, but first they gave themselves to the Lord and to us [as His representatives] by the will of God [disregarding their personal interests and giving as much as they possibly could].
AMP.6: So we urged Titus that, as he began it, he should also complete this gracious work among you as well.
AMP.7: But just as you excel in everything, [and lead the way] in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in genuine concern, and in your love for us, see that you excel in this gracious work [of giving] also.
AMP.8: I am not saying this as a command [to dictate to you], but to prove, by [pointing out] the enthusiasm of others, the sincerity of your love as well.
AMP.9: For you are recognizing [more clearly] the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [His astonishing kindness, His generosity, His gracious favor], that though He was rich, yet for your sake He became poor, so that by His poverty you might become rich (abundantly blessed).
AMP.10: I give you my opinion in this matter: this is to your advantage, who were the first to begin a year ago not only to take action [to help the believers in Jerusalem], but also [the first] to desire to do it.
AMP.11: So now finish this, so that your eagerness in desiring it may be equaled by your completion of it, according to your ability.
AMP.12: For if the eagerness [to give] is there, it is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what he does not have.
AMP.13: For it is not [intended] that others be relieved [of their responsibility] and that you be burdened [unfairly], but that there be equality [in sharing the burden]—
AMP.14: at this present time your surplus [over necessities] is going to supply their need, so that [at some other time] their surplus may be given to supply your need, that there may be equality;
AMP.15: as it is written [in Scripture], "He who gathered much did not have too much, and he who gathered little did not lack."
AMP.16: But thanks be to God who puts the same genuine concern for you in the heart of Titus.
AMP.17: For Titus not only accepted our appeal, but was so very interested in you that he has gone to visit you of his own accord.
AMP.18: And we have sent along with him the brother who is praised in the gospel [ministry] throughout all the churches;
AMP.19: and not only this, but he has also been appointed by the churches to travel with us in regard to this gracious offering which we are administering for the glory of the Lord Himself, and to show our eagerness [as believers to help one another].
AMP.20: We are taking precaution so that no one will [find anything with which to] discredit us in our administration of this generous gift.
AMP.21: For we have regard for what is honorable [and above suspicion], not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
AMP.22: We have sent with them our brother, whom we have often tested and found to be diligent in many things, but who is now even more diligent [than ever] because of his great confidence in you.
AMP.23: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker in your service; and as for the [other two] brothers, they are [special] messengers of the churches, a glory and credit to Christ.
AMP.24: Therefore, show these men, in the sight of the churches, the proof of your love and our reason for being proud of you.

MSG.1: Now, friends, I want to report on the surprising and generous ways in which God is working in the churches in Macedonia province.
MSG.2: Fierce troubles came down on the people of those churches, pushing them to the very limit. The trial exposed their true colors: They were incredibly happy, though desperately poor. The pressure triggered something totally unexpected: an outpouring of pure and generous gifts.
MSG.3: I was there and saw it for myself. They gave offerings of whatever they could—far more than they could afford!—
MSG.4: pleading for the privilege of helping out in the relief of poor Christians.
MSG.5: This was totally spontaneous, entirely their own idea, and caught us completely off guard. What explains it was that they had first given themselves unreservedly to God and to us. The other giving simply flowed out of the purposes of God working in their lives.
MSG.6: That's what prompted us to ask Titus to bring the relief offering to your attention, so that what was so well begun could be finished up.
MSG.7: You do so well in so many things—you trust God, you're articulate, you're insightful, you're passionate, you love us—now, do your best in this, too.
MSG.8: I'm not trying to order you around against your will. But by bringing in the Macedonians' enthusiasm as a stimulus to your love, I am hoping to bring the best out of you.
MSG.9: You are familiar with the generosity of our Master, Jesus Christ. Rich as he was, he gave it all away for us—in one stroke he became poor and we became rich.
MSG.10: So here's what I think: The best thing you can do right now is to finish what you started last year and not let those good intentions grow stale.
MSG.11: Your heart's been in the right place all along. You've got what it takes to finish it up, so go to it.
MSG.12: Once the commitment is clear, you do what you can, not what you can't. The heart regulates the hands.
MSG.13: This isn't so others can take it easy while you sweat it out. No, you're shoulder to shoulder with them all the way,
MSG.14: your surplus matching their deficit, their surplus matching your deficit. In the end you come out even.
MSG.15: As it is written, Nothing left over to the one with the most, Nothing lacking to the one with the least.
MSG.16: I thank God for giving Titus the same devoted concern for you that I have.
MSG.17: He was most considerate of how we felt, but his eagerness to go to you and help out with this relief offering is his own idea.
MSG.18: We're sending a companion along with him, someone very popular in the churches for his preaching of the Message.
MSG.19: But there's far more to him than popularity. He's rock-solid trustworthy. The churches handpicked him to go with us as we travel about doing this work of sharing God's gifts to honor God as well as we can,
MSG.20: taking every precaution against scandal. We don't want anyone suspecting us of taking one penny of this money for ourselves.
MSG.21: We're being as careful in our reputation with the public as in our reputation with God.
MSG.22: That's why we're sending another trusted friend along. He's proved his dependability many times over, and carries on as energetically as the day he started. He's heard much about you, and liked what he's heard—so much so that he can't wait to get there.
MSG.23: I don't need to say anything further about Titus. We've been close associates in this work of serving you for a long time. The brothers who travel with him are delegates from churches, a real credit to Christ.
MSG.24: Show them what you're made of, the love I've been talking up in the churches. Let them see it for themselves!

NIV.1:
Generosity Encouraged
And now, brothers, we want you to know about the grace that God has given the Macedonian churches.
NIV.2: Out of the most severe trial, their overflowing joy and their extreme poverty welled up in rich generosity.
NIV.3: For I testify that they gave as much as they were able, and even beyond their ability. Entirely on their own,
NIV.4: they urgently pleaded with us for the privilege of sharing in this service to the saints.
NIV.5: And they did not do as we expected, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us in keeping with God’s will.
NIV.6: So we urged Titus, since he had earlier made a beginning, to bring also to completion this act of grace on your part.
NIV.7: But just as you excel in everything—in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in complete earnestness and in your love for us—see that you also excel in this grace of giving.
NIV.8: I am not commanding you, but I want to test the sincerity of your love by comparing it with the earnestness of others.
NIV.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, so that you through his poverty might become rich.
NIV.10: And here is my advice about what is best for you in this matter: Last year you were the first not only to give but also to have the desire to do so.
NIV.11: Now finish the work, so that your eager willingness to do it may be matched by your completion of it, according to your means.
NIV.12: For if the willingness is there, the gift is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what he does not have.
NIV.13: Our desire is not that others might be relieved while you are hard pressed, but that there might be equality.
NIV.14: At the present time your plenty will supply what they need, so that in turn their plenty will supply what you need. Then there will be equality,
NIV.15: as it is written: “He who gathered much did not have too much, and he who gathered little did not have too little.”
NIV.16:
Titus Sent to Corinth
I thank God, who put into the heart of Titus the same concern I have for you.
NIV.17: For Titus not only welcomed our appeal, but he is coming to you with much enthusiasm and on his own initiative.
NIV.18: And we are sending along with him the brother who is praised by all the churches for his service to the gospel.
NIV.19: What is more, he was chosen by the churches to accompany us as we carry the offering, which we administer in order to honor the Lord himself and to show our eagerness to help.
NIV.20: We want to avoid any criticism of the way we administer this liberal gift.
NIV.21: For we are taking pains to do what is right, not only in the eyes of the Lord but also in the eyes of men.
NIV.22: In addition, we are sending with them our brother who has often proved to us in many ways that he is zealous, and now even more so because of his great confidence in you.
NIV.23: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker among you; as for our brothers, they are representatives of the churches and an honor to Christ.
NIV.24: Therefore show these men the proof of your love and the reason for our pride in you, so that the churches can see it.

NRSVCE.1: We want you to know, brothers and sisters, about the grace of God that has been granted to the churches of Macedonia;
NRSVCE.2: for during a severe ordeal of affliction, their abundant joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
NRSVCE.3: For, as I can testify, they voluntarily gave according to their means, and even beyond their means,
NRSVCE.4: begging us earnestly for the privilege of sharing in this ministry to the saints —
NRSVCE.5: and this, not merely as we expected; they gave themselves first to the Lord and, by the will of God, to us,
NRSVCE.6: so that we might urge Titus that, as he had already made a beginning, so he should also complete this generous undertaking among you.
NRSVCE.7: Now as you excel in everything — in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in utmost eagerness, and in our love for you — so we want you to excel also in this generous undertaking.
NRSVCE.8: I do not say this as a command, but I am testing the genuineness of your love against the earnestness of others.
NRSVCE.9: For you know the generous act of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, so that by his poverty you might become rich.
NRSVCE.10: And in this matter I am giving my advice: it is appropriate for you who began last year not only to do something but even to desire to do something —
NRSVCE.11: now finish doing it, so that your eagerness may be matched by completing it according to your means.
NRSVCE.12: For if the eagerness is there, the gift is acceptable according to what one has — not according to what one does not have.
NRSVCE.13: I do not mean that there should be relief for others and pressure on you, but it is a question of a fair balance between
NRSVCE.14: your present abundance and their need, so that their abundance may be for your need, in order that there may be a fair balance.
NRSVCE.15:
As it is written, “The one who had much did not have too much,
and the one who had little did not have too little.”
NRSVCE.16: But thanks be to God who put in the heart of Titus the same eagerness for you that I myself have.
NRSVCE.17: For he not only accepted our appeal, but since he is more eager than ever, he is going to you of his own accord.
NRSVCE.18: With him we are sending the brother who is famous among all the churches for his proclaiming the good news;
NRSVCE.19: and not only that, but he has also been appointed by the churches to travel with us while we are administering this generous undertaking for the glory of the Lord himself and to show our goodwill.
NRSVCE.20: We intend that no one should blame us about this generous gift that we are administering,
NRSVCE.21: for we intend to do what is right not only in the Lord’s sight but also in the sight of others.
NRSVCE.22: And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found eager in many matters, but who is now more eager than ever because of his great confidence in you.
NRSVCE.23: As for Titus, he is my partner and co-worker in your service; as for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.
NRSVCE.24: Therefore openly before the churches, show them the proof of your love and of our reason for boasting about you.

NET.1: Now we make known to you, brothers and sisters, the grace of God given to the churches of Macedonia,
NET.2: that during a severe ordeal of suffering, their abundant joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in the wealth of their generosity.
NET.3: For I testify, they gave according to their means and beyond their means. They did so voluntarily,
NET.4: begging us with great earnestness for the blessing and fellowship of helping the saints.
NET.5: And they did this not just as we had hoped, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and to us by the will of God.
NET.6: Thus we urged Titus that, just as he had previously begun this work, so also he should complete this act of kindness for you.
NET.7: But as you excel in everything — in faith, in speech, in knowledge, and in all eagerness and in the love from us that is in you — make sure that you excel in this act of kindness too.
NET.8: I am not saying this as a command, but I am testing the genuineness of your love by comparison with the eagerness of others.
NET.9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you by his poverty could become rich.
NET.10: So here is my opinion on this matter: It is to your advantage, since you made a good start last year both in your giving and your desire to give,
NET.11: to finish what you started, so that just as you wanted to do it eagerly, you can also complete it according to your means.
NET.12: For if the eagerness is present, the gift itself is acceptable according to whatever one has, not according to what he does not have.
NET.13: For I do not say this so there would be relief for others and suffering for you, but as a matter of equality.
NET.14: At the present time, your abundance will meet their need, so that one day their abundance may also meet your need, and thus there may be equality,
NET.15: as it is written: “The one who gathered much did not have too much, and the one who gathered little did not have too little.”
NET.16: But thanks be to God who put in the heart of Titus the same devotion I have for you,
NET.17: because he not only accepted our request, but since he was very eager, he is coming to you of his own accord.
NET.18: And we are sending along with him the brother who is praised by all the churches for his work in spreading the gospel.
NET.19: In addition, this brother has also been chosen by the churches as our traveling companion as we administer this generous gift to the glory of the Lord himself and to show our readiness to help.
NET.20: We did this as a precaution so that no one should blame us in regard to this generous gift we are administering.
NET.21: For we are concerned about what is right not only before the Lord but also before men.
NET.22: And we are sending with them our brother whom we have tested many times and found eager in many matters, but who now is much more eager than ever because of the great confidence he has in you.
NET.23: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker among you; if there is any question about our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, a glory to Christ.
NET.24: Therefore show them openly before the churches the proof of your love and of our pride in you.
